mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive return direction in > 55% of cases over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day trading horizon during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in > 55% of occurrences across rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates reflect stronger aggregate economic output and corporate revenue fundamentals, which consistently predict positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in < 50% of high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, which forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.
